mnemonics: S.W.E.A.T. - Software is: S oftware (not hardware), W eb-based, E mbedded, A pplication, T echnical (complex). This reminds you of the five facets of software's nature.
keyPoints: Software is an instruction set that provides desired features, function, and performance.
Software is developed or engineered, not manufactured in the traditional sense.
Software doesn't 'wear out' but it does deteriorate due to increasing entropy and changing environments.
commonMistakes: Thinking software can be built using the same mass-production techniques as physical goods (manufacturing vs. engineering).
Believing that once a software is deployed, it remains perfect forever (ignoring the inevitability of change and deterioration).
